sections.paragraph:
κατακλυσμός, οῦ, ὁ (s. prec. entry; Pla.+; Marm. Par. [III b.c.]: 239 Fgm. A 4 Jac. [Deucalion]; Celsus 1, 19; 4, 11; PMagd 28, 18 [III b.c.]; BGU 1121, 27; LXX; En; Test12Patr; GkBar 4:10; SibOr 3, 109; Philo, Joseph.; Just., A II, 7, 2; Tat. 39, 2) flood, deluge, in our lit. only of the flood in Noah’s time (Gen 6–9; Jos., Ant. 1, 92f; 10, 147, C. Ap. 1, 130; Nicol. Dam.: 90 Fgm. 72 Jac. [in Jos., Ant. 1, 95]; Berosus: 680 Fgm. 6 Jac. [in Jos., Ant. 1, 158]; SibOr 4, 53; Iren., Hippol., Orig., Did.; Theoph. Ant. 2, 30 [p. 172, 4] πρό κ.; 3, 16 [p. 236, 18] ἀπὸ κ.) Mt 24:38f; Lk 17:27. κατακλυσμὸν ἐπάγειν bring a flood (Gen 6:17) τινί upon someth. 2 Pt 2:5.—DELG s.v. κλύζω. M-M. Sv.

prec. prec. = preceding

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Celsus Celsus , in Origen, Contra Celsum, II a.d.—List 5

PMagd PMagd = Papyrus de Magdôla—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

GkBar GkBar , s. GrBar—List 2

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Berosus Berosus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 518
